manuscript.title: Working notebook, the child welfare book
manuscript.author: Ryan Mitchell, MSW
manuscript.summary: A research and drafting tracker for the slow book. Chapters as nodes, source notes attached, everything de-identified. The rule is simple: no real case ever leaves this file by name.

chapter.focus: What a home visit actually is, minute by minute, stripped of the procedural language.

sourceNotes.note: Composite of a first visit where the kitchen told the whole story before anyone spoke.
On the gap between what a checklist asks and what a room reveals.

chapter.focus: How harm gets defined, and who pays for where the line lands.

sourceNotes.note: State maltreatment rates by county, the spread that nobody explains out loud.
Poverty mistaken for neglect, again and again.

chapter.focus: Removal, and the part of the day that does not end at five o'clock.

sourceNotes.note: The drive home after. The journal exists for exactly this.

chapter.workingTitle: The Other Side of the Table
chapter.focus: Birth families as people, not case numbers. What the cohort taught me here.

sourceNotes.note: Foster cohort discussion: working with birth families, not against them.

chapter.focus: Secondary trauma as a clinical concept and a lived one. No lectures, just the truth.

sourceNotes.note: Therapy as maintenance, not crisis. The Monday slot that holds the week.

chapter.focus: The system is broken in important ways and worth working inside anyway. Where it could actually change.

sourceNotes.note: National data trends over a decade. What the numbers start to say.

milestones.milestone: Reorganized source notes, de-identification pass complete.
Three chapters in clean draft by year end.

manuscript.throughline: Children need to be told the truth in the language they can carry. Everything in the book serves that one sentence.
